summary: `emit` silently drops an authored body on a `json-document` member — `project_bytes` renders fields alone, and `plugin-manifest` is body-bearing, so authored prose vanishes at a `.json` path

edit.description: `project_bytes` (1734) takes the projection's `body` and, on the `Some(Format::JsonDocument)` arm (1740-1742), returns `write_document(fields)` — `body` is never read there. Every other arm renders it. So a `json-document` member's authored prose is dropped from its projection with no error: invariant 3 ('temper never rewords, synthesizes, or drops authored words') and invariant 6 ('no path silently degrades ... or emits over an unresolved input') both break on the same arm. This is the sibling of the bug f8d2431 fixed — that tick made `format` load-bearing on the write face and reasoned that install's `placements` cannot exist over JSON bytes *by construction* (`placement_lines` matches only a `---\n` prefix or a banner marker), which is sound; it did not reason about `body`, which has no such construction argument. **The fix is a refusal, not a render** — a JSON document has no prose slot to render into, so the honest act is to refuse loudly rather than invent a home for the words. Site it where the refusal can carry a `Result` and fire before any byte is written (`specs/model/pipeline.md`, 'Emit': refusal precedes output): the `Projection`-building loop (985-991, where `format: format_from_row(facts)?` is already lifted at 988 and where the include resolution already refuses) is the natural home; `project_bytes` itself returns `String` and cannot refuse without a signature change. Add the `DriftError` variant beside `IncludeNotUtf8` (238) / `IncludeArity` (252) — same shape: a `#[diagnostic(code(...))]`, the member's `kind:name` address, and a message naming the member and its format, carrying the reason: the words have no home in this format, so route them to a body-bearing member or drop them at the source. `project_bytes`'s doc comment asserts the JSON face 'takes no `placements` and drops nothing' — true of placements, false of `body` today; it moves with the change.

The format's own home (its header: 'the format's own faces, held apart from any roster'), and no other entry opens it — so the acceptance belongs here rather than in a new file. Add the falsifier: a `json-document` payload member carrying a non-empty body refuses, naming the member, and writes no bytes; the empty-body case (every fixture today) still emits byte-identically, so the refusal cannot be satisfied by breaking the shipped path. Drive the include case too if it costs little — it is the vivid one: `splice_includes` (983) resolves the include, pushes its `include_row` into the lock, and then the spliced bytes are dropped, so temper fingerprints a dependency whose content reaches no artifact.

tests.asserts: a `json-document` member with a non-empty body refuses before any byte is written, naming the member; an empty-body member emits its canonical document unchanged

acceptance: No authored word reaches a `json-document` projection's write face and vanishes: a body that cannot be rendered is an error at author-time, never a silent drop (`specs/intent.md`, invariant 3 'Verbatim prose' and invariant 6 'Loud or nothing' — the per-surface refusal clauses are instances of that rule, and a surface without one is a gap, not a license). `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: Sweep finding, window 14c5de4..4084dcd. Reachable today, not theoretical: `pluginManifest` (`sdk/src/builtins.ts:589`) declares no `shape`, so it is body-bearing (`content` absent = `file`, one verbatim prose body), and `MemberInit` carries `prose?` (`sdk/src/kind.ts:197`) unconditionally — the SDK type refuses nothing. `blockedBy` TYPE-PREDICATE-ROUND-TRIPS on `src/drift.rs` alone; nothing else is shared. scoped at 21e003a.

summary: `temper bundle`'s two hard-coded `serde_json` manifest writers reconcile onto the shipped kinds — emit through the kind system, one home

edit.description: `run()` (178) writes `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (185) and `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (191) through `write_json` (278) over the hand-built `plugin_manifest()` (223) and `marketplace_manifest()` (238) values — every cite re-verified unmoved on disk at 21e003a. **Note what is NOT duplicated:** `write_json` already delegates to `crate::json_manifest::write_manifest`, so the JSON *encoder* has one home already and is not this entry's target. What is duplicated is the manifest *values*: hand-built `BTreeMap`s spelling a shape the kind system types once `plugin-manifest` (shipped c68f625) and `marketplace` ship. The manifests become members of their kinds, rendered by the kind's format, so `bundle` stops hand-spelling a shape the engine can now type and gate. This is the consolidation decision 0031 names ('bundle's hard-coded writers reconcile onto the kinds — emit through the kind system, one home; the entry names the unification'), and `specs/process/engineering.md` makes it the entry rather than scope creep — name the unification in the commit body. NOT in scope: the `SessionStart` hook's `hooks/hooks.json` (`hooks_manifest()`, 259) and the skill's `SKILL.md` — `hooks.json` is a plugin-cache artifact of neither kind, and widening to it is the creep the same page forbids. If any part of either manifest's shape cannot route through a kind, say so plainly rather than leaving a silent hand-rolled remainder.

The bundle's existing acceptance. Its assertions over both manifests are the regression net that makes this a true refactor: the emitted bytes should not move, and if they do, that is a behavior change to state out loud in the commit body, never a snapshot to bless quietly.

tests.asserts: `bundle` emits both `.claude-plugin/` manifests through their kinds' formats, byte-identically to the pre-refactor output, and the emitted manifests pass their own kinds' default contracts — the product checks what it writes

acceptance: The two manifest writers have one home: `bundle`'s output is its members rendered by their kinds' formats, not hand-built `serde_json` beside a kind that types the same shape (`specs/process/engineering.md`, 'One job, one home': a job the tree already performs is extended where it lives, never reimplemented beside itself). The bundle temper publishes passes the contract temper ships for it, closing the gap decision 0031 named — the product wrote files it refused to check. `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: Derived from decision 0031 (16ce347), the third derivation item. Blocked on `marketplace` alone now — `plugin-manifest` shipped at c68f625. It consumes EMIT-JSON-DOCUMENT-WRITE-FACE's dispatch rather than building it: that shipped at f8d2431, verified on disk. Distinct from the `(plugin-author-dogfood)` fork: `bundle` still assembles its tree into an output dir, and no `.claude-plugin/` is committed here. scoped at e81c758; cites re-verified 21e003a.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. All cites re-verified unmoved at 21e003a. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (65) sits under a doc (61-64) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (627) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (654), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 525 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 624 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 649-652 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1356, re-verified unmoved at 21e003a) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1357-1361) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1359) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 21e003a and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics; the inbox is empty and the window (f8d2431, c68f625) touched no import mechanics. `src/graph.rs` untouched by that window: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` still reads 5 at 65 under a cite claiming five, and 525/624/654 plus `tests/graph.rs:1356` are all unmoved. Stays parked: the constant may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified 21e003a.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 21e003a, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. Channel 3's publish is the release act; BUNDLE-EMIT-THROUGH-KINDS is the orthogonal question of how those bytes are produced. scoped at b676656.
